summary: Solid, well-structured contract with 24 testable criteria covering both the doctor command and error polish features. All five doctor checks from spec 3.12 are individually specified with pass and fail scenarios. Error polish criteria sample across multiple commands rather than trying to exhaustively enumerate every permutation, which is practical. Architecture and testing meta-criteria round out coverage. No revision needed.

criteriaAssessment.comment: Good entry-point criterion. 'At least 5 checks' is specific and verifiable. Top-level registration check via --help is a smart inclusion.
Covers both pass and fail paths. Actionable message requirement (Xcode command-line tools) is specific enough to verify.
Straightforward check with clear pass/fail scenarios.
Good. The spec mentions 'offer to launch if not running' — correctly deferred in outOfScope.
Specific about the expected fix guidance (System Settings > Privacy & Security > Automation). Testable.
Tests both the count display and the zero-accounts failure path. Good.
Critical UX criterion — no short-circuit on failure. Test steps verify multiple failures coexist in output.
Clean exit code contract. Tests single and multiple failure cases.
JSON schema is specific: checks array with name/status/message per check, top-level all_passed boolean. Parseable contract for programmatic consumers.
Lightweight but useful. Ensures --json is discoverable via help.

Code-inspection method is appropriate for verifying message format patterns across error classes. Note: ScriptTimeoutError currently says 'Mail.app may be unresponsive' without a remedy action — this criterion correctly asks for one to be added.

Broad scope ('all commands') but test steps are specific about what to look for: render_error/handle_mail_error usage, exit code 2 vs 1 distinction. Code inspection is the right method for this — runtime testing every error path in every command would be impractical.
Sampling 3 commands is a reasonable strategy for verifying consistency. The pattern 'not found' + known accounts list is specific and verifiable.
Tests two commands (messages list and messages move). Guidance to use 'mailctl mailboxes list' is a nice UX touch.
Sampling 3 commands for message-not-found consistency. Testable.
Tests both messages list (empty mailbox) and messages search (no matches). Clear expected output.
CliRunner stdin simulation can be tricky but the criterion is testable — invoke with no --body, no --body-file, and empty stdin input. The 'mentions at least two alternatives' threshold is practical.
Simple, specific, testable. File path in error message is the key assertion.
Verifying no ANSI escape codes is straightforward. Good to test this on the new command specifically.
Architecture criterion ensures the doctor command follows established project conventions. Checks register() pattern, output utility imports, and independent check testability.
'At least 11 test cases' is specific and the enumerated scenarios cover the full matrix. Good meta-testing criterion.
'At least 4 test cases' across account-not-found, empty-results, and attachment-not-found. Reasonable coverage bar.
Full suite pass is the baseline. Catches unintended breakage from error polish changes.
Explicit regression criterion. --help check for the new doctor command is a nice touch to verify it coexists with existing commands.

featureCoverage.doctorCommand: Fully covered. All 5 checks from spec 3.12 (osascript, Mail.app installed, Mail.app running, scriptable, accounts) have individual criteria (C-221 through C-225). Output format (C-226), exit codes (C-227), --json (C-228), --help (C-229), --no-color (C-238), and architecture (C-239) are also covered.
featureCoverage.errorPolish: Well covered via sampling strategy. Consistent error format (C-230), render_error usage (C-231), account-not-found (C-232), mailbox-not-found (C-233), message-not-found (C-234), empty results (C-235), missing body (C-236), and missing attachment (C-237) cover the full error taxonomy.

regressionCoverage: Lists all 8 prior sprints. Note: Sprint 5 is listed in regressionSprints but is absent from harness-state/regression/registry.json — the evaluator should rely on running the full pytest suite (C-242) to cover Sprint 5 criteria in lieu of explicit registry entries.
outOfScopeAssessment: Reasonable deferrals. Auto-launching Mail.app, network connectivity checks, localisation, and retry logic are all fair to exclude. Shell completions, integration tests, and help text polish are correctly attributed to Sprint 10.
safetyAssessment: Doctor command is entirely read-only — safe to run against real Mail.app during evaluation. Error polish is string/formatting changes only. No send paths, no destructive operations. Safety notes in the proposal are accurate.
